raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("cytokines related to periapical lesions")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the gathered evidence about cytokines related to periapical lesions, I notice that multiple studies emphasize the significance of TNF-α along with IL-6 in triggering severe inflammatory responses and contributing substantially to the breakdown of periapical tissues. While IL-6 indeed regulates bone metabolism and contributes to inflammation, the evidence strongly supports the inclusion of TNF-α given its powerful effect on inducing strong immunity-driven damage typical in these lesions. Therefore, my revised stance aligns closely with choosing option D - TNF-α as a critically important cytokine in periapical lesion biology. \(\boxed{{D}}\) Agreeing slightly modified emphasis towards stronger support for TNF-α than initial conclusion did.
